comments.text:
👇 Everything we curated for this Patagonia episode. Tours, books, cookbooks, travel resources.

*📚 BROWSE THE PATAGONIA COLLECTION*
  ◦ Every book, cookbook, and cultural item we'd recommend → https://amzn.to/4wZ4XNk

*🛍 SEE OUR FULL W.O.E. STOREFRONT*
  ◦ Every destination Collection in one place → https://www.amazon.com/shop/whereonearth

*🎟 TOURS AND EXPERIENCES IN PATAGONIA*
  ◦ Perito Moreno, Torres del Paine, El Chaltén, and the Beagle Channel → https://shorturl.at/pplUn
  ◦ Browse all Patagonia experiences → https://shorturl.at/jNM9E

*📖 PLAN YOUR PATAGONIA TRIP*
  ◦ Moon Patagonia 6th Edition by Wayne Bernhardson → https://amzn.to/4nSqsLq
  ◦ Lonely Planet Latin American Spanish Phrasebook 9 → https://amzn.to/4u6rmph

*📚 GO DEEPER INTO PATAGONIA*
  ◦ Patagonia: A Cultural History by Chris Moss → https://amzn.to/4a2gt0p
  ◦ In Patagonia by Bruce Chatwin → https://amzn.to/4uMCusI

*🍲 BRING PATAGONIA HOME*
  ◦ Seven Fires: Grilling the Argentine Way by Francis Mallmann → https://amzn.to/4xdhuNk
  ◦ Authentic Argentine yerba mate gourd and bombilla set → https://amzn.to/4fbmsU8

comments.author: @Mostyxes
comments.text: The 9000 year old hand gesture is a lie. Stating that everyone uses their right hand is also a lie. On the right hand wall, they use their left hand. The painting is a modern doodle. The devil wants ice to be found for profit. Nazi work.

comments.author: @Jetmab04
comments.text:
Absolutely stunning and, definitely on my list to visit before I die ❤❤❤
Argentina and Chile just leave this beautiful place as it is and, the sad black old priests just stay in their Vatican..."amen"..

comments.author: @balkan_trip
comments.text: Full of wrong information and imagery in the very first minute...

comments.author: @Noah-l6l1t
comments.text: Truly extraordinary! And a deeply tragic indigenous tale!

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: Glad you enjoyed the journey! Thanks for watching!

comments.author: @EpicAdventureWorld
comments.text: Would you rather explore Patagonia for 1 month with no internet… or spend the same time in Europe’s biggest cities?

comments.author: @dodi21955
comments.text:
Remarkable!! And such a sad indiginous story! Let Patagonia be it's own country!! Give it back to the native Towhelché descendants along with the Welsh and the condors! 
Have a referendum.

comments.author: @sean-hu2fo
comments.text: It's amazing and disgusting, how many cultures and people's,  Europeans have destroyed and demeaned. Every culture that they met, they were welcomed and taught things which helped them to survive. Only to turn around and destroy the helpful indigenous  people's, later on!

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: What a thing to have seen it with your own eyes. Thank you for sharing that!

comments.author: @twalatka
comments.text: I really love your channel. Such a relaxing change to most media. May God bless you.

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: Thanks for the love! More journeys to come! Stay tuned!

comments.author: @NickPalfrey8
comments.text: There's a 2010 film called Patagonia featuring a Welsh couple visiting the region and an Argentinian grandmother going to Wales. Very good movie.

replies.author: @WhereonEarth4K
replies.text: That film is a wonderful companion piece — the same journey from a completely different angle. The Welsh-Patagonia connection runs deeper than most people expect. 🌿

replies.author: @danamiddleton4320
replies.text: Thanks !  I'll check out the film.

comments.author: @EpicEarthYTB
comments.text: Patagonia’s landscape is breathtaking! It's fascinating to learn how nature and diplomacy intertwined to prevent a major conflict in 1978.
